releases.title: v3.1.5
releases.summary: This patch release makes library detail drawers feel more content-shaped and less template-driven after the earlier library and profile cleanup passes. Books, collectibles, events, comics, TV seasons, and the shared media drawers now use calmer hierarchy, quieter actions, and more truthful source treatment, while newer library tabs also persist correctly across refresh.

details.heading: Book drawer cleanup
bullets: Reworked book drawers into a wider, less machine-shaped layout with calmer title treatment and labeled source links.
Reduced provider-plumbing exposure so raw URLs, duplicated destinations, and low-value technical metadata no longer dominate the book detail surface.
Tightened the lower-half arrangement for editions, rating, and notes so the drawer feels less bottom-light.

details.heading: Collectible and event drawer restraint passes
bullets: Flattened collectible and event drawers rather than redesigning them into heavier panel systems.
Replaced louder button-like external actions with quieter links where appropriate and toned down footer controls.
Tightened spacing and information hierarchy so these sparser drawers feel denser and more intentional without becoming overdesigned.

details.heading: Shared media drawer normalization
bullets: Normalized shared media drawers for movies, TV, games, and comics so titles, notes, external actions, and footer controls no longer feel like leftovers from the older button-heavy drawer system.
Corrected provider/source labeling so games point to `View on IGDB` instead of inheriting movie/TV labels, and media actions read more truthfully by destination.

details.heading: Comic drawer cleanup
bullets: Suppressed visible internal and provider IDs in comic drawers.
Stopped leaking Calibre and OPDS plumbing into `Type Details` and converted provider links into clearer labels such as `View on Metron` and `Download on Calibre`.
Added a restrained long-overview treatment so oversized comic summaries collapse behind a simple show more/show less toggle.

details.heading: TV season cleanup and shared-library persistence fix
bullets: Replaced stacked nested TV season cards with a flatter season-tab treatment using shorter `S1`-style labels and quieter watched-state feedback.
Fixed newer library tabs like events and collectibles so they persist correctly across refresh instead of falling back before feature flags finish loading.

releases.title: v3.1.4
releases.summary: This patch release reshapes account management into a calmer, more intentional shell surface after the library cleanup from `3.1.3`. The profile page, account menu, and profile-image flow now feel more integrated with the rest of the app, and platform and homelab were brought back onto the same refreshed shared UI path where intended.

details.heading: Profile surface cleanup
bullets: Reshaped the profile page into a calmer account-management surface with cleaner spacing, flatter supporting treatments, and a more intentional identity summary.
Reduced the boxed, generic account-page feel so the profile page sits more naturally inside the shell.
Kept the milestone focused on account/profile experience instead of widening into broader auth or settings redesign.

details.heading: Consolidated account navigation
bullets: Replaced the duplicated footer-account treatment with one clearer account entry point in the shell.
Standardized the account menu contents around `My profile`, `Discord`, `GitHub`, and `Sign out`.
Tightened the profile/account entry so navigation feels more deliberate and less duplicated across the shell.

details.heading: Profile image support and auth/profile persistence
bullets: Added profile-image support across the profile surface and shell account entry.
Simplified the avatar interaction into a clickable upload target instead of a separate preview box and direct URL field.
Persisted `users.image_path` through auth/profile handling and added the follow-on schema/migration repair needed so `/api/auth/me` and profile edits recover cleanly on the running stack.

details.heading: Edition UI alignment
bullets: Rebuilt homelab and platform onto the same refreshed shared shell and library-adjacent UI path so comics, search, pagination, nav, and profile/account updates land consistently across both editions.
Preserved intended feature-level differences while removing presentation drift caused by stale frontend/runtime divergence.

releases.title: v3.1.3
releases.summary: This patch release cleans up the shared library workflow after the `3.1.2` shell pass by simplifying pagination, clarifying bulk-selection behavior, and flattening valuation presentation. The collection surface now reads more clearly during day-to-day browsing and bulk management without widening back into broader shell or drawer redesign work.

details.heading: Pagination and footer control cleanup
bullets: Replaced the older verbose `Previous` and `Next` footer wording with quieter directional pagination controls.
Tightened the page-size and results-footer treatment so navigation feels lighter and less repetitive.
Kept pagination behavior intact while reducing the amount of interface copy users have to parse.

details.heading: Bulk-selection behavior cleanup
bullets: Removed the idle `Bulk Actions` framing and clarified the difference between selecting the current page and selecting all matching results in the active library type.
Moved selection state into the results header so it reads as content-region state instead of a second heavy toolbar.
Kept escalation from page selection to full-result selection available without overloading one ambiguous `Select all` affordance.

details.heading: Valuation presentation cleanup
bullets: Simplified valuation display in library detail drawers into a straightforward `Low` / `Mid` / `High` row.
Reduced the visual weight of the refresh affordance so valuation reads like normal metadata rather than a noisy utility widget.
Kept the valuation treatment compact so it supports the drawer instead of dominating it.

details.heading: Supporting regression alignment
bullets: Carried the browser-regression contract correction for the space-manager flow so fallback manageable-workspace behavior is asserted correctly after suspension or removal with surviving unrelated workspace access.

releases.title: v3.1.2
releases.summary: This patch release tightens the post-split shell experience without reopening shared-core architecture work. The auth page now uses a simpler single-column layout, and the Help and Help Admin surfaces carry less narration and less panel-heavy chrome, so platform and homelab feel more consistent and less overdesigned.

releases.title: v3.1.1
releases.summary: This patch release closes the confidence gap between the shared-core lifecycle work from `3.1.0` and what users actually see in the browser. The Playwright regression layer now proves browser-visible fallback and boundary behavior for support sessions, library lifecycle fallout, and space lifecycle fallout, so the extracted shared-core repair paths are no longer validated only by backend/runtime smokes.

releases.title: v3.1.0
releases.summary: This minor release turns the proven edition boundary into a more explicit product split by tightening the shared core that both platform and homelab consume. Shared auth, scope, library, and lifecycle behavior now follow one more disciplined runtime contract, while the platform and homelab shells are kept distinct where their product responsibilities differ.

details.heading: Shared-core extraction and scope normalization
bullets: Tightened shared auth/bootstrap behavior so effective scope resolution is derived more consistently from active library and accessible membership state instead of stale persisted workspace hints.
Consolidated more support-session, active-scope, and active-library normalization into shared helpers instead of leaving route-local repair logic scattered across auth and lifecycle flows.

Reduced drift between request-level effective scope, persisted profile state, and session-auth payload shaping so `/api/auth/me`, scope payloads, and related shared routes align on the same derived state.

details.heading: Lifecycle repair hardening
bullets: Extracted more library-access-loss and space-access-invalidation behavior into common service helpers so archive, delete, ownership transfer, suspension, and membership-removal flows reuse one repair path.
Tightened cleanup of stale active-library, support-library, current support-session, and previous support-session pointers when users lose access to a library or space.
Added targeted backend/runtime coverage to prove the extracted helpers repair real lifecycle fallout on the live stack.

details.heading: Edition split and shell boundary tightening
bullets: Made the edition contract more explicit in shared bootstrap/auth behavior so platform and homelab consume one authoritative definition of their allowed surfaces.
Continued separating platform-only route groups and shell behaviors from homelab-safe shared surfaces instead of relying on raw role checks or hidden-but-mounted behavior.
Hardened the local maintainer workflow so platform and homelab run as first-class parallel compose targets during split verification rather than toggling one shared stack back and forth.

details.heading: Public homelab split preparation
bullets: Kept the private platform control plane intact while tightening the shared core needed for a later public homelab repo promotion path.
Focused this release on safe extraction, contract hardening, and edition verification rather than public repo publishing mechanics.

releases.title: v3.0.0
releases.summary: This major release completes the frontend build modernization from Create React App to Vite. The app now builds and runs through a single Vite-based toolchain for local development and production static assets, the nginx-served frontend image uses Vite output directly, the legacy CRA fallback rail is removed, and the repo has been re-proven against the live Docker stack and browser-regression suite on the final Vite-only state.

releases.title: v2.11.0
releases.summary: This minor release adds the first optional market valuation foundation for collectZ. Workspace-scoped integrations can now carry valuation provider configuration, media records can store normalized read-only value bands, the library detail drawer can display and manually refresh valuation data, and imports can queue non-blocking follow-on valuation jobs for newly created media. The milestone keeps pricing optional, read-only, and safely decoupled from core catalog CRUD and import flows.

releases.title: v2.10.17
releases.summary: This patch release finishes the workspace-owned member lifecycle foundation. Workspace owners and admins can now force password resets, suspend and restore members within the workspace boundary, invite a brand-new person to become the initial owner during workspace creation, and remove a member from a workspace without silently deleting the content they contributed there. The release keeps platform-global administration distinct from workspace-scoped lifecycle control and preserves the intended tenancy model.

releases.title: v2.10.16
releases.summary: This patch release turns the SaaS auth surface into a real public account-entry flow. Users can now request password resets from the sign-in screen, self-register when the platform allows it, verify their email before normal sign-in, and land in the correct workspace model depending on how they were onboarded. Public self-registration now creates a personal workspace after verification, while invite-based onboarding still lands a new user only in the invited workspace without creating an extra personal one.
